news.title: Embassy REIT raises ₹1,400 crore through 10-year NCD issue
news.para: Embassy REIT has locked in ₹1,400 crore through a 10-year bond sale, enhancing its debt maturity timeline. The initiative saw full subscription from a leading life insurance player, making it the REIT's second successful 10-year bond issuance this fiscal year.

news.title: Barratt Redrow CEO announces retirement, Dean Banks named as successor
news.para: Barratt Redrow has made a significant announcement with the appointment of Dean Banks as the incoming chief executive, effective late 2026. Until March 2027, David Thomas will assist in the transition, ensuring stability amidst fluctuating market conditions facing UK homebuilders.

news.title: Nashik civic body brings 30,000 new properties under tax net
news.para: The Nashik Municipal Corporation has added approximately 30,000 new properties to its tax rolls, pushing the total above 6 lakh. This retrospective assessment, covering at least two previous financial years, has generated an additional Rs30 crore. The civic body anticipates earning Rs15 crore annually from these newly taxed units.

news.title: IISc study flags groundwater crisis in Bengaluru; BWSSB readies ₹10 crore summer plan
news.para: Bengaluru faces a looming groundwater crisis, with 65 wards reporting alarming depletion. A Rs 10-crore plan is underway to mitigate potential summer shortages, including intensified borewell monitoring and ensuring optimal functioning of RO plants. The city water supply agency is also prioritizing new connections and repairing damaged pipelines to address the issue.

news.title: Over 75% Mihan PAPs given developed plots: Maharashtra CM
news.para: Developed plots are being handed over to Mihan project-affected persons in Nagpur district. Over 75% have received their plots. More than Rs100 crore has been paid to 2,048 individuals as compensation for promised employment. Land acquisition for the Mihan project began in 1997. Farmers were promised compensation, plots, and jobs.

news.title: Karnataka high court rules property buyer entitled to khata transfer
news.para: A property buyer under the SARFAESI Act is entitled to a khata transfer, the high court ruled. The court noted that a sale certificate under the Act carries a presumption of regularity. It directed the restoration of power supply and consideration of the khata transfer application, stating that alleged unauthorized construction couldn't justify khata cancellation beyond statutory limitations.

news.title: Maharashtra government scraps NA tax for urban housing societies, waives all dues
news.para: Maharashtra government has abolished the annual non-agricultural tax for urban housing societies. This decision offers significant financial relief to thousands of flat owners across the state. All pending dues and arrears have been waived. The move simplifies procedures and resolves long-standing disputes. This change applies to all urban residential properties, old and new.

news.title: Pune civic body demolishes illegal constructions spread over 78,000 sq ft in Lohegaon, Nagar Road areas
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation demolished 18 illegal constructions spanning 78,000 sqft in Lohegaon and Nagar Road areas. The drive targeted both residential and commercial properties, including Rameshwaram Cafe in Vimannagar, for violating building permission rules. Action was taken after owners failed to comply with notices issued under the MRTP Act.

news.title: SC orders CBI inquiry into DLF's housing project in Gurugram
news.para: The Supreme Court has directed the CBI to conduct an inquiry into issues related to the ‘The Primus’ housing project in New Gurgaon, with findings to be submitted by April 25, 2026.

news.title: UK: Vistry shares sink as it expects profit margins to fall in 2026
news.para: Shares of Vistry, a prominent British homebuilder, have taken a notable hit. The company is bracing for tighter profit margins in 2026, driven primarily by sales incentives. As CEO Greg Fitzgerald prepares to retire, Vistry is strategically positioning itself to enhance sales and liquidity.

news.title: Modis Navnirman to redevelop residential society in Mumbai
news.para: Modis Navnirman will redevelop the New Chitra Co-operative Housing Society in Kandivali West. The existing 14-storey building will be demolished. A new commercial-residential tower will be constructed on the 2,300 sq meter plot. This project is expected to generate ₹160 crore in gross development value. Modis Navnirman is also developing another project in Borivali West.

news.title: Embassy REIT acquires 0.3 million sq ft office space in Bengaluru for ₹852 crore
news.para: Embassy Office Parks REIT has bought a Bengaluru office property for ₹852 crore. The deal involved acquiring Eleanor Realty Holdings India, which owns the Pinehurst building. This grade-A office space is fully leased to a global investment firm. The transaction was completed at a discount to independent valuations. PwC and Trilegal provided advisory services for the deal.

news.title: Focus of Ahmedabad development plan shifts to 2041
news.para: Ahmedabad Urban Development Authority is drafting a 20-year Development Plan (DP 2041), a departure from the traditional 10-year cycle, driven by the prospect of hosting the 2036 Olympics. This extended plan aims to transform Ahmedabad into a global metropolitan city, with special provisions for sports facilities and infrastructure development in surrounding villages.

news.title: SC issues notice, orders status quo on Ramodaya housing project permissions in Mormugao
news.para: The Supreme Court has issued a notice to Goa's town and country planning department and the Mormugao planning authority regarding permissions granted for a housing society project. The court has also ordered a status quo on the property's possession and construction, following allegations of illegal hill cutting and violations of development regulations.

news.title: Credai moves Madras HC against construction ban near Pallikaranai
news.para: Credai has approached the Madras High Court challenging a National Green Tribunal order that prohibits construction within 1km of Pallikaranai marshland. The association argues the 'zone of influence' was arbitrarily fixed without scientific study or stakeholder consultation. The NGT order has led to a planning freeze across a vast, already developed area.

news.title: Pune civic body mandates sensors to monitor STPs in private housing societies
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation (PMC) has mandated nearly 750 housing societies to install sewage treatment plant (STP) sensors for efficient monitoring. This initiative aims to curb untreated wastewater release and reduce water pollution, with performance data to be monitored centrally. The move supports PMC's broader efforts to enhance sewage treatment capacity across the city.

news.title: Delhi: NDMC tells Palika Dham residents to vacate premises by March 7
news.para: New Delhi Municipal Council has issued an immediate evacuation notice for Palika Dham residents, declaring the building unsafe. A March 7 deadline is set for vacating the premises, with alternative accommodation offered through bidding. This directive follows a similar order in July, which residents had resisted.

news.title: Consumer commission orders Aditya Builders and Developers to execute deed of sale
news.para: A consumer commission has ordered Aditya Builders and Developers to execute a sale deed for a flat bought by Santosh and Swati Joshi in 2004. The firm must comply within two months. Failure will allow the couple to get the sale executed through the commission. The firm also faces a Rs 50,000 compensation order for delays and unresolved sewage issues.

news.title: Noida: Paramount Developers clears ₹106 crore dues, unlocks registry for 135 flats
news.para: Paramount Developers has paid over Rs 106 crore to Noida and Greater Noida authorities, clearing dues for two housing projects. This payment enables the registry of 135 flats, with 35 in Paramount Emotions and 100 in Paramount Floraville. The developer deposited the amount 'under protest' to facilitate buyer registrations.

news.title: Godrej Properties acquires 11 acre land in Gurugram; wins bid for five-acre land in Kolkata
news.para: Godrej Properties has secured land in Gurugram and Kolkata for new residential projects. The Gurugram acquisition spans 11.36 acres and is projected to generate over ₹4,500 crore in revenue. In Kolkata, a five-acre parcel off EM Bypass is set for premium residential development with an estimated revenue potential of ₹1,650 crore.
